task_completion.description: The agent switched to the Sketcher workbench and created a new sketch.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sketcher workbench is active (the workbench dropdown at the top says 'Sketcher') and a sketch grid with axes is visible in the main 3D view.

task_completion.description: A rectangle is drawn in the sketch.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Four connected lines forming a rectangle shape are visible on the sketch grid in the 3D view.

task_completion.description: A horizontal distance constraint of 50 mm is applied to the rectangle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A horizontal dimension line is attached to the top or bottom edge of the rectangle with the text '50 mm' (or '50') visible.

task_completion.description: A vertical distance constraint of 30 mm is applied to the rectangle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A vertical dimension line is attached to the left or right edge of the rectangle with the text '30 mm' (or '30') visible.
The lines of the rectangle are light green (FreeCAD's default color for fully constrained geometry) and/or the Task panel on the left displays the message 'Fully constrained sketch'.

task_completion.description: The document is saved as sketch_model.FCStd.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD window title bar at the very top, or the root node in the Model tree view (Combo View on the left), displays 'sketch_model' or 'sketch_model.FCStd'.

integrity.description: The agent used the FreeCAD graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen clicking on toolbars, menus, and the 3D view. There should be no evidence of the agent bypassing the GUI by typing Python commands into a terminal or the FreeCAD Python console to instantly generate the sketch.

integrity.description: The dimensions applied are genuine FreeCAD sketch constraints.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 50mm and 30mm dimensions appear as standard FreeCAD Sketcher constraint objects (typically red or orange dimension lines with text) rather than floating text annotations or image overlays.

integrity.description: The file was saved genuinely through the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: A 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog box is visible in at least one screenshot, showing the file path '/home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/sketch_model.FCStd' being entered, or the application title bar updates naturally after a save action.
